title: Quiz: Vulnerable Systems
description: Test your understanding of systems vulnerable to quantum attacks

questions.question: Which TLS component is NOT directly broken by Shor's algorithm?
questions.explanation: AES-256-GCM is a symmetric cipher, which is only affected by Grover's algorithm (weakened but not broken). ECDHE, RSA, and ECDSA are all broken by Shor's algorithm.

questions.question: Why are cryptocurrency wallets with exposed public keys particularly vulnerable?

options.text: The private key can be computed from the public key using Shor's algorithm

questions.explanation: Bitcoin and Ethereum use ECDSA. Once a public key is exposed (which happens when you spend from an address), a quantum computer running Shor's algorithm can compute the private key and steal remaining funds.

questions.question: What makes critical infrastructure systems particularly challenging to secure against quantum threats?

options.text: Long operational lifetimes (20-40 years) and difficult update processes

questions.explanation: Critical infrastructure like power grids and water systems have equipment with 20-40 year lifespans and very difficult update processes. Systems installed today need to remain secure for their entire operational life.

questions.question: Why are software supply chain attacks particularly dangerous in a post-quantum world?

options.text: A single forged code signing key can compromise millions of systems
Only government systems use code signing

questions.explanation: Code signing certificates are used to verify software authenticity. If an attacker can forge signatures (trivial with Shor's algorithm), they can distribute malware that appears legitimate to millions of systems.

questions.question: SSH is only vulnerable in its key exchange; host key verification and user authentication remain quantum-safe.
questions.explanation: False. SSH uses RSA, ECDSA, or Ed25519 for host keys AND user authentication, all of which are broken by Shor's algorithm. The key exchange (ECDH/DH) is also broken. All core SSH security mechanisms are vulnerable.

questions.question: What is the typical data retention requirement that makes healthcare records particularly vulnerable to 'harvest now, decrypt later' attacks?
questions.explanation: Healthcare records typically must be retained for the patient's lifetime plus additional years (varies by jurisdiction). This means data encrypted today may need to remain confidential for 50+ years.

questions.question: Which system would have the HIGHEST priority for quantum migration based on the assessment framework?

options.text: Nuclear facility control systems with 40-year lifespan

questions.explanation: Nuclear facility control systems combine critical data sensitivity, very long operational lifespan (40 years), difficult update processes, and use of Shor-vulnerable algorithms. This creates the highest risk score.

questions.question: Which of the following are accurate statements about financial system quantum vulnerabilities? (Select all that apply)

options.text: SWIFT network messages can be forged with quantum computers
Credit card EMV chips use quantum-resistant cryptography
Stock exchange order signing would be compromised
ATM networks rely on Shor-vulnerable algorithms

questions.explanation: SWIFT uses RSA (forgeable), stock exchanges use digital signatures for orders (forgeable), and ATM networks use RSA/ECC for authentication (broken). EMV chips use RSA and ECC, which are NOT quantum-resistant.
